patches.summary: Patch topic/compare/mispriced and add Polymarket+Kalshi discovery helpers to close the 16 gaps surfaced by live dogfooding (NBA WCF, Kanye, NBA draft, World Cup).
patches.reason: topic FTS query AND-joined hyphenated terms and the BM25-only ranker buried high-volume series markets, dropping participants from multi-outcome events. Kalshi sync wrote list-shape payloads with null prices. compare and mispriced returned opaque empty results on no-pair. The /public-search proxy missed Polymarket celebrity hub topics. Discovery commands (polymarket event-of, polymarket siblings, kalshi-series-search) were missing.
patches.validated_outcome: topic kanye returns the same hit count as topic kanye-west; topic 'NBA Western Conference Finals' surfaces KXNBAWEST; topic 2026-world-cup force-includes USA; polymarket siblings will-ghana-win-the-2026-fifa-world-cup includes the USA market with non-null yesPercent; kalshi sync backfills active high-volume markets; compare returns structured unpaired diagnostic on no-pair; mispriced skips untraded; new commands registered.
patches.upstream_issue: Pending: file against mvanhorn/cli-printing-press to backport ranker + discovery shape into the generator templates.

patches.summary: Apply journal_mode=WAL and busy_timeout=5000 via sqlite.RegisterConnectionHook because DSN-level _journal_mode/_busy_timeout params are silently ignored by modernc.org/sqlite v1.37.0.
patches.reason: Live store opened with the DSN-style pragma params still reported PRAGMA journal_mode=delete and busy_timeout=0, which is the root cause of the SQLITE_BUSY failures observed during concurrent topic queries and during kalshi sync. Connection hook is the only reliable path.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store/... passes (76 tests including 4 new concurrency tests). Live db now reports journal_mode=wal, busy_timeout=5000.
patches.upstream_issue: Generator template should switch from DSN-pragma to connection-hook approach for all printed CLIs that use modernc.org/sqlite.

patches.summary: Add --with-markets to `kalshi events get` (passes with_nested_markets=true upstream) and implement --series on `kalshi events list` (advertised in --help pre-fix but never declared as a flag).
patches.reason: Before this patch the only way to enumerate every child market under a Kalshi event was raw curl against the upstream API. The CLI now returns all 56 KXMENWORLDCUP-26 child markets in one call. The --series filter on list completes the series → event → markets walk that the existing CLI surface implied but couldn't actually execute.
patches.validated_outcome: End-to-end smoke against live Kalshi API: `kalshi events get KXMENWORLDCUP-26 --with-markets --agent` returns 56 markets including KXMENWORLDCUP-26-PT; `kalshi events list --series KXMENWORLDCUP --data-source live --agent` returns the 2026 event. go test passes; verify_skill.py still passes after SKILL.md additions.

patches.summary: Add --venue, --polymarket, --kalshi flags to topic, compare, liquid, movers, resolving, trending, new via a shared addVenueFlags + resolveVenue helper. Default behavior (no flag) stays cross-venue.
patches.reason: Agents that already know which venue they want should be able to skip the second one to halve query latency. compare additionally errors when scoped to a single venue (it requires both).
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes. verify_skill.py still passes.

patches.summary: Refresh price-bearing fields from upstream APIs at command time for topic / compare / mispriced / trending / movers / resolving / liquid / new. Surface index age and refresh outcome in meta.price_source and meta.index_synced_at.
patches.reason: The local SQLite store is a discovery index but doubled as the price source. A user asking 'what are the odds Lakers win tonight' could be served hours-old cached prices with no signal they were stale. The refresher batches hits by venue and fires one /markets?slug= (Polymarket) or /markets?tickers= (Kalshi) call per venue, replacing cached values on the in-memory hits before serialization. A failed live fetch degrades affected rows to price_source: stale rather than failing the whole command. Complements the existing kalshi sync-time price backfill (which fills nulls in the cache) by ensuring the displayed price reflects current upstream state at command time.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes with new freshness assertions. agent-context schema_version bumped with new top-level freshness block enumerating the commands that emit meta.price_source. Human-mode terminal output prints a 'Index synced Xh ago, prices live' footer; --json / --agent mode does not.
patches.upstream_issue: Generator template for CLIs that maintain a local discovery index alongside a price-bearing upstream should adopt the live-on-read pattern by default.

patches.summary: Generate a per-command --select cheatsheet from JSON-tagged response structs and expose it via agent-context.commands.<name>.select_paths. Adds tools/select-paths-gen/, the generated internal/cli/select_paths.go, a drift-guard test, and a `which <cmd> --select-paths` flag. agent-context schema bumped 5->6.
patches.reason: Agents passing --select today guess dotted paths against response shapes they have not introspected; a wrong path returns {} silently (not an error), forcing re-fetches that burned ~25% of wall-clock on two of three smart-learning failure traces. The CLI already knows its response shapes (typed Go structs with JSON tags) — this patch surfaces them at agent-context time so an agent's first --select call lands. Per docs/plans/2026-05-23-002 section U7.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (451 tests, 11 new across select_paths_test.go covering pinned commands, slice/pointer/embedded expansion, nested meta envelope, sort+unique invariants, agent-context round-trip, JSON marshal shape, and a generator drift guard). agent-context --pretty surfaces commands.topic.select_paths (22 entries) and commands.markets.subcommands[get-by-slug].select_paths (140 entries including question/slug/outcomes/outcomePrices/lastTradePrice/bestBid/bestAsk/volume/liquidity/endDate). `which markets get-by-slug --select-paths` dumps the same set. Running `go generate ./internal/cli/...` from either the CLI root or internal/cli/ produces zero diff.
patches.upstream_issue: Generator template should consider emitting per-CLI select-paths cheatsheets via the same build-time AST walk; pattern generalizes to any printed CLI with typed response structs.

patches.summary: Add `teach` (LLM-fired, silent, backgroundable) and `recall` (LLM-fired, pre-discovery) plus a rerank layer in topic/compare that applies the resulting boost/hide/alias rules. schema bumped 3->4 with the search_learnings table; agent-context schema bumped 4->5 to advertise the contract.
patches.reason: The agent paid the 'took forever to find Portugal's ticker' tax on every session because the central FTS index has no signal that the LLM already found the right ticker last time. Now the LLM teaches the CLI at response-time with the user's natural-language question and the tickers that made it into the answer; future overlapping queries (token-set Jaccard >= 0.6) short-circuit discovery via `recall` and rerank surfaces the right tickers via the apply layer. Per-user, fire-and-forget, silent on success, inspectable via `learnings list`, undoable via `forget`, and disableable via --no-learn / PREDICTION_GOAT_NO_LEARN.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (416 tests, 33 new across teach/recall/learnings/forget/apply). verify_skill.py passes. End-to-end: a `teach` for 'portugal world cup odds' lands a row that subsequent `recall` calls find by token overlap; the rerank layer surfaces it at position 0 in `topic` output regardless of FTS ranking; `--no-learn` and PREDICTION_GOAT_NO_LEARN both disable the surface. teach.log captures background failures; learnings.jsonl mirrors every teach/forget for grep-without-SQL inspection.
patches.upstream_issue: Generator template for printed CLIs that ship a discovery index should consider adopting the LLM-driven teach/recall pattern: per-user, fire-and-forget write surface plus pre-discovery read surface, fed by the LLM's act of responding rather than CLI-side heuristics. Generalizes to any read-mostly catalog CLI.

patches.summary: Make recall entity-aware: filter cross-entity matches into a debug-only mismatches array, attach per-row entity_match (exact/partial/mismatch/unknown) and warnings, and rank exact > partial > unknown. Add --debug-mismatches flag. Wire UpsertLearning to persist query_entities at teach time so cold first-recall has the entity signal it needs.
patches.reason: Before this patch the v3 recall returned any Jaccard-positive learning regardless of which entity the resource carried, so 'odds England wins world cup' returned a Portugal-tagged learning at the documented threshold. U3 splits the recall match into Jaccard (token-set, non-entity normalized) AND entity overlap (case-insensitive against the resource's entity-bearing fields), surfacing mismatches separately so the LLM can tell 'good answer' from 'noise that looks like a good answer'. Per docs/plans/2026-05-23-002 section U3.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (500 tests, +34 over baseline 466 across new match/recall surfaces and CLI tests). go vet clean. verify_skill.py passes. End-to-end: a Portugal learning + an England query returns found=false by default; --debug-mismatches surfaces the Portugal row in mismatches[]. A Kalshi parent-event learning with a matching child market keeps entity_match=exact and attaches parent_event_when_child_exists:<child-ticker> as a warning. UpsertLearning now persists query_entities so a fresh teach's first recall has the right entity signal without waiting for a re-migration.
patches.upstream_issue: Same liftability target as the entities + normalizer patches: internal/learn/{match,recall}.go depends only on database/sql + the entities extractor + Normalize; ready to lift into the generator template alongside U1/U2.

patches.summary: Replace lowercase+stopword normalization in the learning subsystem with an entity-preserving normalizer (internal/learn/normalize.go) that splits queries into Entities, Tickers, and a stable NonEntityNormalized form. Schema v3->v4 adds search_learnings.query_entities (JSON array) and backfills existing rows by running the extractor against their stored query_pattern.
patches.reason: The v3 normalizer destroyed entity tokens: 'odds England wins the world cup' collapsed to 'england cup world' and Jaccard-matched a stored Portugal learning at >=0.6, surfacing the wrong ticker on recall. Preserving entities case-intact in a separate slice lets U3's match validator reject cross-entity matches even at high non-entity Jaccard. The Kalshi/Polymarket ticker patterns and prediction-market stopword vocabulary live in internal/learn/ (one layer above the domain-agnostic entities extractor) so the extractor stays liftable into a future generator template.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (454 tests, 15 new: 10 normalize, 5 v3->v4 migration). go vet clean. verify_skill.py passes. The 'odds England wins the world cup' and 'odds Portugal wins the world cup' queries normalize to the same NonEntityNormalized ('cup world') but different Entities (['England'] vs ['Portugal']). A v3 DB with pre-existing search_learnings rows opens cleanly, the query_entities column is added, and every row is backfilled (entity-bearing rows get JSON arrays like '["Portugal"]', stopword-only rows get '[]'). Re-Open is a no-op.
patches.upstream_issue: Pending: the learn package's prediction-goat-specific config (Kalshi/Polymarket ticker patterns + odds/wins/beats stopwords) is the consumer-side wrapper; the domain-agnostic entities extractor (internal/learn/entities) should lift into the generator template so future CLIs that ship a discovery index inherit the entity-preserving Jaccard contract.

patches.summary: Add the entity_lookups table seeded with canonical ISO 3166-1 country codes and current-season major-league sports team aliases (~900 rows), plus a Lookup/LookupAll/Upsert API in internal/learn/lookups and a teach-lookup CLI command for per-user additions. Schema v4->v5.
patches.reason: The recipe inference engine planned for U10 needs canonical entity-substitution data so it can take a learned pattern from one entity (Portugal -> KXMENWORLDCUP-26-PT) and apply it to any other entity in the same kind (England -> KXMENWORLDCUP-26-GB) without re-learning. Shipping the data inside SQLite (instead of a JSON file or an embed.FS scan) lets per-user teach-lookup rows coexist with seeded rows in one table queried through one surface. Computed kinds (lowercase, kebab-case, capitalize-first, uppercase, slug) short-circuit the DB via in-package string transforms — the table holds only canonical reference data, never domain logic. Per docs/plans/2026-05-23-002 section U9; correction from plan: actual on-disk baseline was v4 (not v5), so this bumps v4->v5 not v5->v6.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (501 tests, +35 across lookups CRUD, computed kinds, seed counts, v4->v5 migration, teach-lookup CLI). go vet clean. verify_skill.py passes. Fresh DB at v5 inserts 923 entity_lookup rows: country_iso2 (236), country_iso3 (205), country_lowercase (145), NFL/NBA/MLB/MLS team abbrev+lowercase combined (337). Computed-kind Lookup never touches the DB (nil-DB tests pass). User taught rows coexist with seeded rows under the (kind, canonical, value) PK; INSERT OR IGNORE makes re-seeding idempotent.
patches.upstream_issue: Pending: lift internal/learn/lookups + its seeds subpackage into the generator template as an opt-in 'reference-data' module. The domain-agnostic API surface (Lookup/LookupAll/Upsert/IsComputedKind) plus the seed-file convention (one .go file per domain, []LookupRow at compile time) generalizes to any CLI that benefits from canonical name->code mappings (stock tickers, currency codes, etc.).

patches.summary: Add teach-time resource-shape validator (U6): emits structured warnings to teach.log when an LLM teaches against a Kalshi parent event AND a child market matches the query entity, or when query entities don't overlap with the resource's entities. Teach succeeds silently; warnings are diagnostic. Adds --no-validate flag to teach and --warnings flag to learnings list.
patches.reason: Pre-U6 the parent-vs-child guard fired at recall time, which meant an LLM that taught the parent ticker once never learned to teach the child. The validator runs after upsert, so an offline grep of teach.log surfaces the pattern of LLMs taking the lazy path. Soft warning by design -- a teach against the parent IS legitimate when the query is categorical (no entities), so the validator only fires when the query has entities AND a strictly-better child exists. Per docs/plans/2026-05-23-002 section U6.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (560 tests, +24 across validator/log/CLI). go vet clean. verify_skill.py passes. Flagship replay: teach KXMENWORLDCUP-26 against 'odds USA wins world cup' when child KXMENWORLDCUP-26-US carries yes_sub_title=USA produces a parent_event_when_child_exists JSONL entry in teach.log naming the child. --no-validate suppresses cleanly. learnings list --warnings --agent returns {warnings: [...]} with the same entries, filterable by --resource. Polymarket-side parent detection is intentionally deferred (TODO referenced in teach.go), matching the U6 plan's Deferred section.
patches.upstream_issue: Liftable target: internal/learn/teach.go + teach_log.go depend only on database/sql, the entities extractor, and match.go's IsKalshiParentTicker / ResourceEntities / ClassifyEntityMatch. Ready to lift into the generator template alongside U1/U2/U3 once the broader learn-package template extraction lands.

patches.summary: Add the search_recipes generalization layer: Extract walks recent search_learnings rows, groups them by structural shape, and writes substitute / substitute-then-search-prefix recipes; Apply substitutes the live query's entity into matching recipes and verifies against resources; recall.go merges recipe hits after the direct-lookup pass. Adds the teach-recipe CLI command for explicit template authorship. Schema v5->v6.
patches.reason: Without templating, teaching one country's World Cup ticker (Portugal -> KXMENWORLDCUP-26-PT) leaves every other country needing its own teach. The recipe engine generalizes a learned pattern across the country_iso2 (and other) entity_lookups kinds so a single Portugal+USA teach pair unlocks England, Germany, Brazil, etc. for free. Per docs/plans/2026-05-23-002 section U10; correction from plan: actual on-disk baseline was v5 (not v6), so this bumps v5->v6 not v6->v7.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (562 tests, +26 across recipes CRUD/extract/apply/generalization regression and recall+recipe integration). go vet clean. verify_skill.py passes. Fresh DB at v6 includes search_recipes table; v5->v6 upgrade adds the table via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (no backfill needed). Flagship regression: teach Portugal+USA (Kalshi+Polymarket each), call recall on 'odds England wins world cup' — returns Found=true with both KXMENWORLDCUP-26-GB (substitute) and the England Polymarket slug (substitute-then-search-prefix) tagged source='recipe'. Direct teaches outrank recipe hits within the same entity_match tier.
patches.upstream_issue: Pending: lift internal/learn/recipes into the generator template alongside lookups + entities + match + normalize. The recipe engine is the most-domain-agnostic of the four: it operates on the abstract LearningRow shape, resolves entities via the lookups package's Lookup/IsComputedKind contract, and verifies candidates via the generic resources table. Future CLIs that ship a discovery index inherit the generalization layer by plugging in their own seed lookups + entity extractor config.
